subTopicTitle: Waiting for Godot (Beckett) — Act I — Pozzo & Lucky arrive

items.prompt: Describe the initial entrance of Pozzo and Lucky in Act I of 'Waiting for Godot'.
keyPoints: Pozzo enters driving Lucky with a rope around his neck
Pozzo is portrayed as a master, while Lucky is a subservient character
Their entrance interrupts Estragon and Vladimir's waiting

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Explain the significance of the relationship between Pozzo and Lucky.
keyPoints: Reflects themes of power and control
Demonstrates dependency and dehumanization
Symbolizes broader social hierarchies

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Identify the main themes introduced through Pozzo and Lucky's arrival.
keyPoints: Themes of time and waiting
Themes of existentialism and absurdity
Themes of power dynamics

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: How might Pozzo and Lucky's arrival affect the atmosphere of the play if set in a modern-day context?
keyPoints: Contrast between modern setting and traditional roles
Potential for heightened tension and discomfort
Increased focus on themes of alienation and technology

items.prompt: Imagine Pozzo and Lucky are symbols in a different play. How could their relationship symbolize a different theme?
